data.title: Ethereum onchain volume tops $320 billion in August, highest since mid-2021
data.description:
A string of multi-year peaks across Ethereum's ecosystem has accompanied ETH's August all-time high.

Monthly adjusted onchain transfer volume on the network surpassed $320 billion in August, the highest since May 2021 and the third-largest month on record, according to The Block’s data dashboard. This volume measures economic throughput on the Ethereum blockchain, including transfers, DeFi interactions, and other transactions.

30-day transactions also set fresh highs, while monthly active ETH addresses reached their second-highest point ever, and total value locked remains close to ATH.

The surge in activity coincides with a sharp increase in accumulation by corporate Ether treasuries, increased spot ETH ETF trading, and a multi-year low in average transaction fees.

Corporate digital asset treasuries have been a notable tailwind. Public companies’ cumulative ether holdings climbed from roughly $4 billion in early August to more than $12 billion by month-end, led by large additions from BitMine Immersion and SharpLink Gaming. That balance-sheet demand coincided with a jump in spot ETH ETF volumes to late-month peaks and persistent net inflows across issuers, a sign of investor appetite as the complex now holds over 5% of ether's supply.

Transaction costs on Ethereum are also near a five-year low, supporting higher onchain usage. The March 2024 Dencun upgrade introduced EIP-4844, “proto-danksharding,” which slashed data costs for rollups and pushed more activity to lower-fee Layer 2 networks. This year's Pectra release introduced a positive undertone, featuring an upgrade designed to enhance account abstraction, developer tooling, and user experience. These changes are widely viewed as additive to throughput and usability over time.

Network churn added another layer of activity. Validator exit requests are at a record high while entry requests hit a two-year peak. Industry trackers have tied part of that movement to withdrawal-driven flows into liquid restaking protocols, which have collected tens of billions of dollars this summer as operators optimize yield and liquidity.

Analysts at major banks like Standard Chartered have recently argued that the market is undervaluing Ethereum relative to its growth drivers, including treasury adoption and ETF demand, even as the ETH/BTC ratio has strengthened over the year.

At the same time, a handful of large bitcoin holders have rotated some capital into ETH, adding to the summer’s cross-asset flow narrative. U.S. public sector experiments with posting macro data to public blockchains have also kept attention on Ethereum’s role as a general-purpose settlement infrastructure.

As of Friday afternoon, ETH traded 12% off its all-time high following recent market corrections. The Block’s price page shows ETH changing hands around $4,337, down over 5% on the day.

data.title: Bond Holders Doubt Credible Budget Consolidation in France — Market Talk
data.description: Since France is stuck in political paralysis and with no credible prospect of budget consolidation, the country is quickly losing its privileged status among bondholders, analysts at Oxford Economics say in a note. Bond investors "no longer think that French bonds warrant special consideration simply 'because it's France', as famously put by the former president of the European Commission, Jean-Claude Juncker," the analysts say. Given the political instability is set to persist, "we expect market tensions and volatility to remain high, and French spreads to stay elevated," Oxford Economics says. The 10-year French government bond, or OAT, yield is up 1.9 basis points at 3.500%, while the 10-year Bund yield rises 1.3 basis points to 2.712%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Why these Wall Street banks are bullish on the world's second-largest stock market
data.description:
By Jules Rimmer

A revival in Chinese equities is getting noticed

Chinese shares are trading at ten-year highs and Wall Street is getting excited.

Two heavyweight Wall Street banks have urged investors to join the rally in Chinese stocks, which has seen its main CSI 300 index rise 14% so far in 2025.

With a total stock market capitalization of around $11 trillion, China is the world's second-largest, ranking behind the U.S. only.

Both JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Goldman Sachs upgraded their targets for China's main share indices and recommended a bullish stance, in separate reports published Friday. JPMorgan wrote a separate comment on China earlier this week.

Goldman retained its overweight call, predicting a 10% return on the MSCI China benchmark and 12% return for the CSI 300 XX:000300 index, while JPMorgan was far more optimistic, forecasting 35% potential returns for MSCI China and 24% for the CSI 300 by the end of 2026.

Elsewhere, Citi's macro-strategy team led by Adam Pickett, identified China as a candidate to add risk but taking into account "China suffers from very poor September seasonals," in a note to clients on Friday. His suggestion: "We bide our time."

What Goldman strategist Kinger Lau described as a "liquidity-driven rally," has further to go in his opinion, as the summer breakout develops momentum. Large-cap China A CAF shares have motored 8% in August, while small-caps managed 10% gains. Lau pointed out this strength has occurred despite weak macro data and muted profit revisions for Chinese corporates.

His argument is bolstered by valuation multiples he considers less than demanding, high single-digit trend profit growth, retail risk appetite that is less than excessive thus far, while the same can be said for fund positioning for institutional investors.

Perhaps his most compelling argument , though, is the reallocation of flows from bonds to equities after Chinese government bond yields BX:AMBMKRM-10Y plummeted in the last year or so to just 1.7%. A modest uptick to 1.79% in August implies some investors are already shifting.

Goldman Sachs does not find Chinese stock valuations excessive on mid-cycle, price-earnings multiples.

For Lau then, "the uptrend has legs" and JPMorgan's team, led by strategist Nikolaos Panirgitzoglou, was even more emphatic. He pointed out that institutional flows have been the chief locomotive for the rally in shares since September 2024, but retail punters have risen since July 2025 adding a liquidity tailwind.

JPMorgan also cited the low yields available on bonds, pushing investors into stocks and the confidence they derive from what is considered a "PBOC put," whereby the People's Bank of China (or central bank) will introduce market-supporting measures if they feel stocks are unduly weak.

JPMorgan expects a falling equity risk premium (the additional return investors demand from stocks compared to the perceived safety of fixed-income) to force valuation multiples higher, lending weight to their aggressive index targets. The team recommends investors focus on media & entertainment, information technology, biotech, materials and non-bank financials as sectors for exposure.

Next week, China will hold its Victory Day parade on Sept. 3, commemorating eighty years since victory over Japan in World War II, and JPMorgan suggested this may also generate some positive and patriotic goodwill for trading sentiment.

-Jules Rimmer

data.title: Canada - Aug 29
data.description:
The following are the top stories from selected Canadian newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

THE GLOBE AND MAIL

** Canadian fintech company Planswell is facing complaints over unusual hiring practices, roughly five years after its corporate reincarnation from a robo-adviser into an online platform that connects financial services professionals with people seeking advice.

** The legal battle over the future of 25 former Hudson’s Bay stores went before the Ontario Superior Court on Thursday, with the retailer arguing that B.C. billionaire Weihong (Ruby) Liu should be allowed to take over the leases, while a number of major landlords countered that she would be unfit as a retail tenant.

** Montreal-based, online high-end fashion retailer Ssense plans to file for protection from creditors imminently after its lenders pushed for the sale of the company.

data.title: Andrea Orcel’s Greek foray promises handsome gifts
data.description:
The UniCredit CEO had to abandon an Italian deal and hit a rut in his pursuit of Germany’s Commerzbank. Yet a 26% stake in 8-bln-euro Alpha Bank is already paying off. A full takeover looks viable, and may face less political opposition than Orcel encountered in Rome and Berlin.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @Unmack1 on X

CONTEXT NEWS

UniCredit on August 28 said that it had increased its stake in Greek lender Alpha Bank to 26%, through “additional financial instruments.”

UniCredit said that its total stake in Alpha would generate a return on investment of around 20%, assuming a share in earnings of 244 million euros. It is requesting authorisation from regulators to convert a portion of that stake from derivatives into physical shares, and to consolidate its share of Alpha’s net income.

Alpha Bank shares fell modestly in early trading on August 29.

data.title: Summa Defence's Board establishes Audit and Risk Committee and Nomination and Remuneration Committee
data.description:
Summa Defence Oyj

Summa Defence Oyj

Company Announcement 29 August 2025 at 1:45 pm EEST

The Board of Directors of Summa Defence Plc has decided to establish an Audit and Risk Committee, whose members are Sirpa-Helena Sormunen (Chairperson), Jussi Mälkiä and Erja Sankari.

The Audit and Risk Committee is responsible for, among other things, preparing matters related to the company's financial reporting, internal control and risk management for the Board.

In addition to the Audit and Risk Committee, Summa Defence Plc is establishing a Board Nomination and Remuneration Committee, whose members are Arto Räty (Chairperson) and Antti Vehviläinen. Due to the small number of Board members, only two members have been selected for the Nomination and Remuneration Committee.

The duties of the Nomination and Remuneration Committee include, among other things, assisting the Board in preparing the company's remuneration principles and practices as well as preparing and handling remuneration reports. The Committee shall prepare proposals for the nomination and appointment of the CEO and other members of senior management who report directly to the CEO. The Committee shall not prepare proposals for the nomination of Board members, which falls under the responsibility of the Shareholders’ Nomination Board.

SUMMA DEFENCE PLC

More information:

Jussi Holopainen, CEO

Phone: +358 44 517 4543

Email: jussi.holopainen@summadefence.com

Media contact:

Tommi Manninen, Chief Communications Officer

Phone: +358 400 437 515

Email: tommi.manninen@summadefence.com

Summa Defence in brief

Summa Defence Plc is a Finnish defense and security technology group supporting industry growth and strengthening industrial production capacity amid geopolitical change. Summa Defence focuses on dual-use technologies related to security of supply, situational awareness, mobility, and defense, which benefit the civilian, security, and defense sectors while strengthening comprehensive security, security of supply, and crisis management capabilities.

The shares of Summa Defence Plc are listed on the Nasdaq First North Growth Market in Sweden (SUMMAS) and Finland (SUMMA). www.summadefence.fi/en/

The company’s Certified Adviser is Augment Partners AB, info@augment.se, tel. +46 8-604 22 55.
